title: Create your first circular genome figure
summary: Upload one small GenBank record, choose a circular preset, show feature labels and GC tracks, and export the result without running a sequence search.
requirements: No external search runtime is required.

downloads.label: NC_012920.1
downloads.note: Download the complete record as a GenBank flat file and keep this filename.

manualSteps.title: Select Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Circular. This workflow uses one annotated GenBank file and does not need Pairwise Comparisons.

operations.body: Choose Circular before uploading the record.

manualSteps.title: Upload the GenBank record
manualSteps.body: Upload HmmtDNA.gbk and set Output prefix to HmmtDNA_basic_circular.

operations.body: Check that the HmmtDNA.gbk file chip appears in the Circular GenBank uploader.

media.caption: Upload the human mitochondrial GenBank record.

manualSteps.title: Choose the compact layout
manualSteps.body: Set Track Preset to Middle, enable Separate Strands, set Label Mode to Out, and enter Homo sapiens in Species. Keep the default GC content and GC skew tracks.

operations.body: Choose Middle from Track Preset and keep the default quantitative tracks enabled.

media.alt: Circular Track Preset selector with Middle highlighted.
media.caption: Middle keeps the feature and GC tracks compact on this small genome.

manualSteps.title: Generate and export SVG
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. Check that features, labels, GC content, and GC skew are visible, then use SVG in the export controls.

operations.body: Compare the preview with the Gallery result before exporting.

media.alt: Beginner circular human mitochondrial genome diagram with feature and GC tracks.
media.caption: The completed beginner figure requires no sequence-search step.

related.label: Next: add an AT skew track
